prompts: A lever consists of a rigid beam and is pivoted at a point called the fulcrum. The effort is the force applied to one end of the lever to move the load or resistance at the other end. The load or resistance is the force exerted by the lever at the opposite end of the effort. The placement of the fulcrum, effort, and resistance determine the mechanical advantage gained. The distance between these points affects how much force is needed to lift the load, demonstrating the principle of leverage in physics.
